top_signals.title: Stocks Climb as US-Iran Deal Eases Inflation Angst: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street staged a comeback that saw stocks and bonds climbing after a peace deal between the US and Iran spurred optimism that a revival of the Strait of Hormuz will ease inflation risks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bessent Urged by US Senators to Push China on Undervalued Yuan
top_signals.summary: A bipartisan duo of senators has called on Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to step up cooperation with the Group of Seven to pressure China into addressing what they called a deliberate suppression of its exchange rate for commercial advantage.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Long-End Yields Will End 2026 at Levels Rarely Seen in the Last Decade: Markets Pulse
top_signals.summary: Thirty-year Treasury yields will most likely push back over 5% by the end of the year, the latest Markets Pulse survey showed, signaling some doubts about whether the Federal Reserve will move quickly to enough rein in the recent inflation surge.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bank of Canada Can Let Loonie Weaken and Skip Hikes, BofA Says
top_signals.summary: The Bank of Canada is likely to keep rates on hold through most of 2027 and allow the Canadian dollar to weaken further even if US monetary policy tightens, Bank of America says.
